AAPL — Apple Inc.

Type: Equity  |  Generated: 2026-03-31 00:39

Description

Apple Inc. designs, manufactures, and markets smartphones, personal computers, tablets, wearables, and accessories worldwide. The company offers iPhone, a line of smartphones; Mac, a line of personal computers; iPad, a line of multi-purpose tablets; and wearables, home, and accessories comprising AirPods, Apple Vision Pro, Apple TV, Apple Watch, Beats products, and HomePod, as well as Apple branded and third-party accessories.

Fundamentals

AAPL (Apple Inc.) operates in the Technology sector, Consumer Electronics industry, with a market capitalisation of $3626.27B.

AAPL has a trailing twelve-month P/E ratio of 31.19 and a forward P/E of 26.49. Earnings per share (EPS) for the trailing twelve months is $7.91. Revenue for the trailing twelve months is $435.62B, with net income of $117.78B.

AAPL's gross margin is 47.33%, operating margin is 35.37%, and net profit margin is 27.04%.

AAPL has a debt-to-equity ratio of 102.63 — elevated but common for large-caps with buyback programmes, and a current ratio of 0.97 (current liabilities exceed current assets — watch liquidity). The dividend yield is 0.42% with a payout ratio of 13.04%.

Price & Momentum

AAPL's current price is $246.72, within a 52-week range of $169.21 to $288.62.

AAPL's price returns are -6.50% over the past 30 days, -7.66% over the past 90 days, and +13.72% over the past 12 months.

AAPL is currently -5.1% below its 50-day SMA of $259.98, and -0.5% below its 200-day SMA of $247.94.

AAPL has an RSI (14-day) of 28.04, which is in oversold territory (below 30). Beta relative to the S&P 500 is 1.12 — moderately more volatile than the broader market. Average daily trading volume is 46,586,027 shares.

Historical — last 60 trading days

Trend summary

AAPL moderately declined 9.3% from $272.01 to $246.72 between Jan 02, 2026 and Mar 30, 2026. The period high was $280.65 and the period low was $243.19. The maximum drawdown over the period was -11.21%, measured peak to trough. The sharpest two-week rally of 12.1% centred around Feb 06. Average daily trading volume over the period was 47,557,852 shares.

Volatility

AAPL's annualised realised volatility over the past 60 days is 24.6%. The 14-day ATR for AAPL is $5.03, representing 2.04% of the current price. AAPL recorded 5 trading days with gains exceeding 2% and 5 days with losses exceeding 2% during this period. The best single day was +4.06% on Feb 02, and the worst was -5.00% on Feb 12.

Volume

A volume increase of 1.9× the 60-day average occurred on Jan 30, indicating elevated but not exceptional interest.

AAPL's next earnings release date is not yet announced.

AAPL carries a buy — analysts lean bullish overall among 41 analysts. The mean analyst price target is $295.31, implying 19.7% upside from the current price.
